overview: Performs character counts on different parts of a set of documents, following the [GMX-V 2.0 standard](http://www.xtm-intl.com/manuals/gmx-v/GMX-V-2.0.html). Character counts are saved as annotations on resources at various levels (text unit, batch, document, etc.) and can be consumed by downstream steps such as the Scoping Report Step to generate reports.

countInBatch.help:
Adds a character count annotation aggregated across the entire batch. This annotation accumulates counts from all batch items, documents, and text units within the batch.

A character count annotation is **always** set on each individual text unit regardless of this setting — this option controls whether an additional roll-up annotation is created at the batch level.

countInBatchItems.help:
Adds a character count annotation for each batch item. The count aggregates all documents and text units within that batch item.

A character count annotation is **always** set on each individual text unit regardless of this setting — this option controls whether an additional roll-up annotation is created at the batch-item level.

countInDocuments.help:
Adds a character count annotation for each document. The count aggregates all text units within that document.

Useful when processing multiple documents and you need per-document character totals for reporting or scoping.

countInSubDocuments.help:
Adds a character count annotation for each sub-document. Sub-documents are logical subdivisions within a document (e.g., embedded content or document parts).

Only meaningful when the input filter produces sub-document boundaries.

countInGroups.help:
Adds a character count annotation for each group. Groups are structural containers within a document (e.g., table rows, list items).

Only meaningful when the input filter produces group boundaries.

bufferSize.help:
Controls the size of the internal text buffer used during character counting.

A value of `0` means no explicit buffer size limit (default behavior).
bufferSize.values: `0` = default (no explicit limit). Positive integers set the buffer size in characters.

processingNotes: Character count annotations are **always** created at the text unit level — the boolean options control whether *additional* roll-up annotations are created at higher structural levels (batch, document, group, etc.).
Counts follow the [GMX-V 2.0 standard](http://www.xtm-intl.com/manuals/gmx-v/GMX-V-2.0.html) (Global Information Management Metrics eXchange — Volume).
The generated annotations can be consumed by downstream steps such as the **Scoping Report Step** to produce character count reports.

examples.title: Scoping report with per-document counts
examples.description: Enable `countInDocuments` to get per-document character count annotations, then pipe the output to the Scoping Report Step to generate a report showing character counts broken down by document.
